name: Marie's Peanut Butter Dreams
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt together butter and peanut butter in a bowl.
2. Add granulated sugar and brown sugar to the melted mixture; stir until combined.
3. Beat in eggs one at a time, then stir in vanilla extract.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together ground oatmeal flour, baking soda, and salt.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the butter mixture, stirring until just mixed.
6. Stir in chopped peanuts if desired.
7. Chill the dough for about one hour.
8. Shape chilled dough into 1-inch balls and place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
9. Flatten each ball with a fork dipped in sugar to create a pattern.
10. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about ten minutes, or until edges are golden brown.
Makes about 4-1/2 dozen cookies.
You can substitute vegetable shortening for butter and skip chilling the dough.
